ai_investment_intensity.description: AI capex / revenue ratio and AI mentions in earnings calls

scoring.25: Minimal AI mentions, <0.5% revenue in AI capex
scoring.50: Moderate AI investment, 0.5-2% revenue, some AI mentions
scoring.75: Significant AI investment, 2-5% revenue, frequent AI mentions
scoring.100: Heavy AI investment, >5% revenue, AI is strategic priority

engineering_scale.description: Engineering headcount as proxy for agent fleet potential

scoring.50: Significant cloud presence, primary provider identified

governance_posture.description: Existing governance maturity (compliance frameworks, AI ethics boards, CISO org)

scoring.0: No formal governance beyond basic compliance
scoring.50: Established compliance org, some AI ethics awareness
scoring.75: Dedicated AI ethics board or responsible AI program
scoring.100: Mature AI governance with dedicated team, published principles

regulatory_pressure.description: Sector-level regulatory exposure for AI systems

agent_tooling_signals.description: Public evidence of AI agent adoption (tooling mentions, deployments)

scoring.50: Uses AI coding tools or basic automation
scoring.75: Deploying autonomous agents or multi-agent workflows
scoring.100: Running production agent fleets, publicly discussed

build_vs_buy.description: Historical preference for building vs. buying infrastructure

scoring.0: Builds everything internally (pure tech company with platform team)
scoring.75: Prefers buy, builds differentiating features only
scoring.100: Buys almost everything, minimal internal engineering

budget_availability.description: Tech budget flexibility based on margins and spending patterns

scoring.0: Tight margins (<5%), no discretionary tech budget

build_internally.description: Company has the scale and capability to build governance internally

no_agents.description: Company shows no evidence of AI agent adoption

compliance_only_governance.condition: governance_posture is compliance-only with no operational component
compliance_only_governance.description: Governance is viewed as compliance checkbox, not operational enablement

open_source_only.condition: company primarily uses open-source AI tooling and avoids paid platforms
open_source_only.description: Company culture favors free/open-source tools over paid governance
